chapters.title: The undeniable power of familial ties
chapters.text:
Very few people haven't heard about Joe Biden — the 46th U.S. President and renowned statesman. On the other hand, Valerie Biden Owens is a much more unknown name even though she's been President Biden's loyal companion and adviser throughout his entire career.

Valerie Biden Owens was happy to remain in her brother's shadow for most of her life. It was an unspoken agreement between them, which worked well for both. Valerie and Joe Biden’s secret to a long-lasting partnership comprised two things: 1. They never held grudges against each other. 2. They shared a common worldview and values.

Since early childhood, they spent so much time together (playing, discovering the world, and learning) that they became absolute experts on the subject of each other. They stayed silent in the most critical moments of their shared history because they did not need words to express their mutual trust and understanding.

All relationships require a strong foundation of common morals and principles to be successful.

Having close and profound relationships is an advantage, and it was one of the few that the Bidens had growing up. They didn't have wealth or status, but they had each other and the power of hope; this legacy helped them throughout their careers, and they passed it on to their children.

Valerie Biden Owens' story is a familiar one for many. She grew up in a patriarchal society in the latter half of the 20th century in a middle-class Irish Catholic family. Valerie was the only girl in the family and grew up with three loving brothers — Joe, Jimmy, and Frankie. Their parents always encouraged them to stand up for themselves and each other, and all Bidens deeply cherished this helpful advice. Later, this family maxim motivated Joe Biden to offer his sister a career opportunity that wasn't an option for women at the time.

I was the kid sister whose brother pulled up a chair for her at a table where there had been no room for women. ~ Valerie Biden Owens

chapters.title: The people in your life can build you up
chapters.text:
On November 5, 1945, Valerie Biden was born into a family of two hard-working Americans. Her dad was a strong man with even stronger values, the traditional “head of the family.” On the other hand, her mom was a gentle soul and a beacon of empathy. Together, they were an excellent parenting combination and taught the Biden children everything there was to know about life.

The family was first based in Scranton, Pennsylvania, but later moved to Delaware in search of better job opportunities. Fortunately, this U.S. state became a good home for all of them and, much later, a launching pad for Joe Biden's political career.

There, the Biden children learned to live by an unspoken family code: 1. Tell the truth, no matter what. 2. Stand up to bullies. 3. Treat everyone with dignity. 4. Never despair.

This simple set of rules helped them navigate their lives and became the general basis of Joe Biden's political platform.

The Biden family is an effective team with a unique way of collaborating. Their dynamic is void of competition and usual sibling rivalry; they've built it on trust and love alone. As a result, whichever path any member of the family chose, the rest were ready to jump in and help — no questions asked. That's why, when Joe, the eldest of the four children, decided to hop into the politics game, most of the family followed in his footsteps.

Excessive competition can destroy a relationship, so it's important to celebrate each other's successes and share the disappointments.

They started the political journey together and stood united when Joe was inaugurated as the 46th U.S. President. Between this tremendous victory and his first as the county councilman lay several lifetimes' worth of joys and heartbreaks, wins and failures, happiness and losses. But the important thing is that the Bidens remained a strong coalition through it all.

Our parents drilled it into our heads from birth that we were to take care of one another. In my mom’s words: “Family is the beginning, the middle, and the end. Period.” ~ Valerie Biden Owens

chapters.title: Treasure your independence
chapters.text:
In the fall of 1959, Valerie Biden began her studies at Ursuline Academy, a girls-only Catholic prep school. Her time there was both challenging and rewarding. She received an excellent foundation for her future education but struggled with the social aspect of school.

Valerie wasn't ready for an all-girls academy, coming from a family with three brothers. She was used to spending time with boys and always strived to be more like them, creating distance between her and her classmates. As a result, during these years, Valerie struggled with self-confidence and self-image. Thankfully, her older brother Joe was always close by, reminding her that she was strong and beautiful.

In her senior year of high school, Valerie began to think about college and her future career. Figuring out what she wanted to do with her life proved rather tricky. However, there was one thing she was sure of — she wanted to be independent. At the time, many expected women to marry well and then devote the rest of their lives to family. For ambitious Valerie, this wasn't enough; she wanted a steady career first. Later, she made this conviction the only rule for her daughters — they had to pursue a profession and not depend on their spouses.

Before committing to a relationship, ensure you have something to fall back on, as financial dependency traps too many women in unhappy or abusive relationships.

After much deliberation, Valerie enrolled in the University of Delaware, where Joe was already a sophomore. Her grades were remarkable, and she easily got in.

There, Joe and Valerie spent even more time together. They were each other's confidants and supporters. When Joe graduated and went on to study in law school at Syracuse in 1965, he never forgot about his little sister and even managed to set up a blind date for her. And although the date with Joe's friend Jack Owens turned out to be a total disaster at the time, Valerie would realize many years later that it was the day she first met the love of her life.

During this time, Joe met and married Neilia Hunter, who quickly became a part of the family. She shared the same philosophy as the rest of Bidens — family above all. Valerie's love life was also busy in college — she was dating a fellow sophomore Bruce Saunders, who then went to Vietnam upon graduating.

chapters.title: Follow your heart, not the traditional script
chapters.text:
During her junior year at the university, Valerie took a brief course in education and immediately realized that this was her future career. She had a unique way of establishing connections with her students, ensuring everyone felt included and valued.

After graduation, Valerie started her first full-time job as an eighth-grade social studies teacher in Washington, D.C. It was her first time living alone and so far from the rest of the Biden family. Even though this was scary, she welcomed the challenge with open arms. Valerie was confident that actual growth could only happen outside her comfort zone.

In 1968, after an inspiring year in D.C., Valerie received a job offer back in Delaware at Wilmington Friends, a private school near her hometown, and immediately said yes. The job was perfect: a good school, proactive students, and a young and idealistic teaching staff. But a year into her career there, Valerie noticed that she was paid much less than her male colleagues, even though she was among the best teachers.

When she confronted the principal, he responded that Valerie, unmarried and with no kids, didn't need as much money as men who had to support their families. The blatant pay discrimination was relatively common at the time but shocking, nonetheless. It was “the common sense,” but its logic was skewed and patriarchal.

Constantly challenge and re-examine anything labeled as “common sense.” The current standards often shift and change depending on who dictates them.

In 1969, Valerie's long-distance boyfriend Bruce Saunders returned from Vietnam with an ultimatum: they had to get married, now or never. The decision to say “yes” was more automatic than conscious; it felt like everyone expected her to take this next step. So despite her deeper instincts, Valerie went through with the wedding. A part of her knew that it didn't feel right, but she missed all the warning signs.

Valerie and Bruce Saunders's marriage wasn't ideal, but it wasn't bad either. They were both loyal and honest people who respected each other, and, though not long-lasting, their relationship was healthy and balanced.

chapters.title: It takes courage to begin a new journey
chapters.text:
Around that time, in the late 1960s, Joe Biden returned to Delaware with a law degree and began working at the public defender's office. He met Sid Balick, a respected attorney who encouraged him to run for an open seat on the New Castle County Council in 1970. This step was the first on the path of politics for Joe Biden and his sister, whom he asked to run the campaign.

Valerie and Joe were completely unprepared to tackle this challenge, but they were optimistic, persistent, and ardent believers in doing the right thing. Combined with a lot of hard work and person-to-person politicking, this turned out to be more than enough to win that race. Though it was a small-scale victory, it laid the foundation for all the future ones and established their campaign rhythm and the specific Biden approach.

Because the money was tight and neither Joe nor Valerie had any resources for the campaign, they had to improvise. Valerie enlisted the help of her students at Friends School, who were enamored with Joe Biden's passion and fresh ideas. They became the powerful force that helped spread the word about the previously unknown candidate and his policies.

As a councilman, Joe Biden became central in the process of the Democratic party's rebirth and redefinition. The party's values were rapidly shifting, and they needed new faces. Joe was the perfect candidate, so they encouraged him to run for the U.S. Senate. It was a monumental challenge for the two siblings, who were still unsure of what they were doing. But neither of them could miss out on the opportunity, and they began their first journey to Washington.

90% of success is simply showing up and having the courage to rise to the challenge.

The choice to run for the U.S. Senate was a defining moment in Valerie and Joe’s lives. During the campaign, they, quite literally, turned the whole political game upside down. Their choices were bold and their ideas innovative. No one believed in them or was ready to back their fight, but none of it mattered — they had each other and their ideals. And they won.

There’s something exhilarating about being the underdog, and we embraced our freedom. ~ Valerie Biden Owens

chapters.title: Tragedy chisels the character
chapters.text:
When Joe Biden was about to start his work as U.S. Senator in 1972, a horrible tragedy hit the lives of the Biden family. Joe's wife, Neilia Hunter, and their three kids were involved in a massive road collision. Neilia and Amy, their youngest child, died in the hospital, and Joe's two sons, Hunter and Beau, were critically injured. The family's grief was inconceivable. It is still difficult for Valerie to talk about that night many years later. The tragic accident nearly shattered the Biden family, but it made them cherish each other and their close bonds even more.

Deep trauma influences our minds and memories in choosing what to bury in the subconsciousness and what to remember forever.

After the painful events, Valerie and her husband moved in with Joe and his two kids. Beau and Hunter needed support, attention, and motherly love, and their aunt Val was the perfect candidate for the job. She left her career as a teacher and devoted all her time to the two boys. The Biden family worked together to support Joe and keep him going through these dark times. Thanks to them, he didn't give up and continued his valuable work.

The stressful changes also tested Valerie's marriage with Bruce Saunders. In those uncertain days, they realized that the time of their marriage was up. The decision to split up was mutual, and they remained close friends for the rest of their lives. Though the divorce felt like a total failure for Valerie at the time, it was the new beginning she desperately needed.

Coincidentally, Jack Owens, a failed blind date from college years, reappeared in Valerie's life. As Joe's loyal friend, he dropped everything to help out after the accident. At first, Valerie and Jack were apprehensive and unsure of each other — they had less than perfect experiences with previous romantic relationships and were wary of committing again. But the mutual trust and honesty between them and the new bond they developed during that time pulled them to each other despite everything.

Their love was sudden, unexpected, and intense. It became the very thing that saved Valerie and pulled her from the pits of depression. With Jack Owens's help, she regained her sense of self and became a firm believer in her own power.

chapters.title: Bidens - the defenders of the American dream
chapters.text:
On October 11, 1975, Valerie Biden officially became Valerie Biden Owens. She dedicated the following years to her family and gladly took on a new role of a wife and mom. But she never quite forgot the excitement of campaigning, and when the time came for Joe's reelection to the Senate, she jumped back into the game.

The 6th youngest U.S. senator in history, Joe Biden, had a long and prolific career in the Senate. He was re-elected six times, regularly receiving over 60% of the votes. Valerie Biden Owens successfully managed each of these reelection campaigns.

After 36 eventful years in the Senate, the Biden coalition was ready to move on to bigger challenges. As a result, in January 2007, Joe Biden and his sister declared his candidacy in the upcoming 2008 presidential race. Unfortunately, he lost the 2008 primary election to Barack Obama. Later though, Obama reached out to Joe Biden and offered him the opportunity to join his presidential campaign as a vice-president candidate. He wanted Joe on board because of his uniquely rich experience. It was a valuable opportunity, and Joe Biden never regretted saying yes to this partnership.

Progress and success are never straightforward; great opportunities and outcomes might arise from what first appears to be a failure.

After the 2008 victory, Joe Biden spent eight fruitful years as the U.S. Vice President. For Valerie, this period meant a break from ongoing political campaigns. Though she loved managing campaigns and was an expert on the subject at this point, she never considered running one for someone else.

Instead, she embarked on her own journey, trying out new things and becoming an agent of change. Among the most valuable experiences were her work with Women's Campaign International and as a Resident Fellow at Harvard University. Both allowed Valerie to share her invaluable knowledge and encourage other women to pursue careers in male-dominated fields.

Political campaigning reappeared in Valerie's life in 2019 when her brother decided to run for president once again. They were confident in his win because this candidacy was about much more than running for office; it was a testament to their family's life-long values. When Joe Biden decided to run against Donald Trump, he stood up to a bully and fought for the very soul of America.

Did you know? More people voted in the 2019 presidential election than ever in U.S. history.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
When Joe Biden was inaugurated as the 46th U.S. President, it was a win for all Bidens. But perhaps most of all, it was a win for Valerie. In 1970 she took a chance on her brother and dedicated all her efforts to his political career. So seeing him in the Oval Office and imagining the endless possibilities of his presidency felt like a satisfying culmination of her professional journey as well as his.

Valerie Biden Owens' life and her partnership with her brother are unique and fascinating examples of determination and perfect teamwork. Her journey was adventurous, and experiences varied: there were painful and crashing blows for every share of good fortune. But Valerie's ability to persevere through it all and continue to trailblaze her path is inspiring and exemplary.

Her story is also a story of courage. Valerie never hesitated when making tough decisions or jumping headfirst into unknown experiences and new challenges. Doing this always came easy because she had a robust support system — her family. Throughout their lives, the Bidens were each other's “true north”; taking care of the family was deeply embedded in their DNA.

Empathy was always central in Valerie Biden Owens' life. It was her guiding light and the basis behind her every decision. And her most important lesson is about empathy as well: simple acts of kindness can transform and heal any man — she hopes they can heal a whole country too.

Kindness reverberates. What you say and do matters. That is what I learned — growing up Biden. ~ Valerie Biden Owens

Try this

• Focus on the relationships within your family and think about how they compare to the Bidens' dynamic. Then, use this opportunity to show some extra appreciation to your family members.
• Follow Valerie Biden Owens on social media to keep up with her journey and learn more about her current work.
• You can also read a summary of Joe Biden’s autobiography, Promises to Keep, on the Headway app to get another perspective on the Bidens’ story.
